ddls:
CREATE TABLE "measurements" (
  "measurement_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each measurement */,
  "station_uid" TEXT /* Identifier for the monitoring station */,
  "record_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for the record set */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the measurement was taken */,
  "anomalous" INTEGER /* Indicates if the measurement is anomalous */,
  "expected_value" REAL /* Expected value within normal operating conditions */,
  "max_value" REAL /* Maximum threshold for the expected value */,
  "min_value" REAL /* Minimum threshold for the expected value */,
  "actual_value" REAL /* Actual recorded measurement value */,
  "sensor_type" TEXT /* Type of sensor used for the measurement */,
  "sensor_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for the specific sensor used */,
  "measurement_unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement */,
  "geolocation" TEXT /* Geographical coordinates (latitude and longitude) of the measurement */,
  "weather_conditions" TEXT /* Weather conditions at the time of measurement */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("measurement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_measurements_station_uid FOREIGN KEY ("station_uid") REFERENCES monitoring_stations ("station_uid")
)

CREATE TABLE "monitoring_stations" (
  "station_uid" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each monitoring station */,
  "station_name" TEXT /* Name of the monitoring station */,
  "location" TEXT /* Geographical location of the station */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date when the station was installed */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current operational status of the station (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude coordinate of the station */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude coordinate of the station */,
  "elevation" REAL /* Elevation of the station in meters */,
  "last_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance or calibration */,
  "contact_info" TEXT /* Contact information for the station */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("station_uid")
)

CREATE TABLE "anomalies" (
  "anomaly_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each anomaly record */,
  "measurement_id" TEXT /* Reference to the measurement that triggered the anomaly */,
  "anomaly_type" TEXT /* Type of anomaly detected (e.g., high value, low value) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the anomaly */,
  "detected_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the anomaly was detected */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the anomaly */,
  "impact" TEXT /* Potential impact of the anomaly */,
  "action_taken" TEXT /* Actions taken in response to the anomaly */,
  "resolved_by" TEXT /* ID of the user who resolved the anomaly */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("anomaly_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_anomalies_measurement_id FOREIGN KEY ("measurement_id") REFERENCES measurements ("measurement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_anomalies_resolved_by FOREIGN KEY ("resolved_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, researcher, analyst) */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the user */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  "active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "anomaly_id" TEXT /* Reference to the related anomaly */,
  "user_id" TEXT /* ID of the user who is notified of the alert */,
  "alert_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was generated */,
  "resolved" INTEGER /* Status of the alert (e.g., resolved, pending) */,
  "alert_level" TEXT /* Level of the alert */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Detailed message of the alert */,
  "resolved_by" TEXT /* ID of the user who resolved the alert */,
  "resolution_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was resolved */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_resolved_by FOREIGN KEY ("resolved_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_anomaly_id FOREIGN KEY ("anomaly_id") REFERENCES anomalies ("anomaly_id")
)
